title: RBI Guidelines on Dispute Resolution Timeline
content:
Dispute Resolution Timeline Requirements:

1. Acknowledgment: Banks must acknowledge customer disputes within 24 hours of receipt
2. Investigation Period: Maximum 30 days for dispute investigation
3. Provisional Credit: Must be issued within 10 business days for fraud claims
4. Final Decision: Must be communicated within 30 days with detailed reasoning
5. Appeal Process: Customer has right to appeal within 15 days of decision

Penalties for Non-Compliance:
- ₹10,000 per day for delays beyond 30 days
- Customer entitled to compensation for undue delays
- Regulatory action for repeated violations

title: RBI Guidelines on Provisional Credit
content:
Provisional Credit Requirements:

1. Fraud Claims:
   - Provisional credit within 10 business days
   - Full disputed amount must be credited
   - Interest on delayed credit at savings rate

2. Merchant Disputes:
   - Provisional credit within 14 business days
   - Applies to disputes > ₹5,000
   - Customer must provide basic evidence

3. Reversal Conditions:
   - Only if investigation proves customer fault
   - Must provide 7 days notice before reversal
   - Customer has right to appeal reversal

4. Documentation:
   - All provisional credits must be documented
   - Audit trail required for 7 years
   - Monthly reporting to RBI

title: RBI Zero Liability Framework
content:
Customer Liability for Unauthorized Transactions:

1. Zero Liability Conditions:
   - Customer reports within 3 days: Zero liability
   - Customer reports within 4-7 days: Liability up to transaction amount or ₹10,000 (whichever is lower)
   - Customer reports after 7 days: Liability as per bank policy

2. Exceptions (Customer Always Protected):
   - Contributory fraud by bank/merchant/service provider
   - Deficiency in bank's security systems
   - Third-party breach where customer not at fault

3. Reporting Requirements:
   - Banks must provide 24/7 reporting channels
   - Immediate card blocking facility
   - SMS/email alerts for all transactions

4. Burden of Proof:
   - Bank must prove customer negligence
   - Customer assumed not liable unless proven otherwise

title: Consumer Protection Act - Right to Appeal
content:
Consumer Rights in Financial Disputes:

1. Right to Appeal:
   - Customer can appeal bank decision within 15 days
   - Appeal must be heard by senior authority
   - Decision on appeal within 30 days

2. Escalation Path:
   - Level 1: Bank's internal grievance officer (15 days)
   - Level 2: Banking Ombudsman (30 days)
   - Level 3: Consumer Court (90 days)

3. Compensation Rights:
   - Mental agony compensation up to ₹1,00,000
   - Litigation costs reimbursement
   - Interest on delayed refunds

4. Documentation Requirements:
   - Bank must maintain complete audit trail
   - All communications must be in writing
   - Customer entitled to copies of all documents

title: RBI Guidelines on Fraud Classification
content:
Fraud Classification and Reporting:

1. Fraud Categories:
   - Card Not Present (CNP) Fraud
   - Card Present Fraud
   - Account Takeover Fraud
   - Social Engineering Fraud
   - Merchant Fraud

2. Reporting Thresholds:
   - All frauds > ₹1,00,000: Report to RBI within 24 hours
   - Frauds ₹25,000-₹1,00,000: Weekly reporting
   - Frauds < ₹25,000: Monthly reporting

3. Investigation Requirements:
   - Police complaint for frauds > ₹50,000
   - Internal investigation for all cases
   - Forensic audit for frauds > ₹10,00,000

4. Customer Protection:
   - Customer not liable if bank security breach
   - Immediate card blocking on report
   - Provisional credit during investigation

title: RBI Guidelines on Merchant Chargebacks
content:
Merchant Chargeback Process:

1. Chargeback Initiation:
   - Customer can initiate within 60 days of transaction
   - Bank must process within 7 days
   - Merchant notified within 3 days

2. Merchant Response Time:
   - 14 days to provide evidence
   - Extension up to 21 days with valid reason
   - Auto-chargeback if no response

3. Evidence Requirements:
   - Proof of delivery with signature
   - Tracking information
   - Customer communication records
   - Terms and conditions acceptance

4. Dispute Resolution:
   - Bank reviews evidence within 7 days
   - Decision communicated to both parties
   - Appeal process available for 15 days

5. Merchant Penalties:
   - Chargeback rate > 1%: Warning
   - Chargeback rate > 2%: Fines applicable
   - Chargeback rate > 5%: Account suspension

title: IT Act - Electronic Transaction Security
content:
Security Requirements for Electronic Transactions:

1. Two-Factor Authentication:
   - Mandatory for all online transactions > ₹5,000
   - OTP validity: Maximum 10 minutes
   - Biometric authentication for high-value transactions

2. Data Protection:
   - Customer data encryption mandatory
   - PCI-DSS compliance required
   - Regular security audits

3. Liability for Security Breach:
   - Service provider liable for security failures
   - Customer not liable if breach proven
   - Compensation for data breach victims

4. Audit Trail Requirements:
   - All transactions logged with timestamps
   - Logs maintained for 7 years
   - Tamper-proof logging systems

5. Penalties:
   - Up to ₹5 crore for data breaches
   - Criminal liability for willful negligence
   - License cancellation for repeated violations

title: RBI Guidelines on Customer Grievance Redressal
content:
Customer Grievance Redressal Mechanism:

1. Internal Grievance Officer:
   - Every bank must appoint dedicated officer
   - Contact details prominently displayed
   - Response within 7 working days

2. Escalation Matrix:
   - Level 1: Branch Manager (7 days)
   - Level 2: Regional Manager (15 days)
   - Level 3: Principal Nodal Officer (30 days)
   - Level 4: Banking Ombudsman (30 days)

3. Communication Requirements:
   - Acknowledgment within 24 hours
   - Status updates every 7 days
   - Final decision in writing with reasons
   - Appeal rights clearly mentioned

4. Compensation Framework:
   - ₹100 per day for delays beyond timeline
   - Maximum ₹5,000 for procedural lapses
   - Additional compensation for mental agony

5. Monitoring:
   - Monthly reporting to RBI
   - Quarterly review by board
   - Annual customer satisfaction survey
